mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5 percentage points within 30 calendar days
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling UNRATE, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real retail sales indicates a supply-demand imbalance where inventory drawdowns force firms to raise prices, compressing equity margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus RSAFS growth) shows no significant correlation with forward SPX returns (p > 0.10) over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or positive returns persist despite widening divergence.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to trigger earnings downgrades across cyclical sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects underlying consumer confidence and disposable income expansion, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change against 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in the industrial production index above its 3-month moving average signals capacity utilization recovery and improved supply chain conditions, reducing input cost inflation and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5 percentage points above its 3-month rolling average
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates a lagged labor market response where firms are hiring but the jobless rate has not yet declined, reflecting structural friction that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS growth > 150k AND UNRATE unchanged or rising) does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or forward returns are positive in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1.0 million annualized units signals weakening construction activity and consumer wealth effects, which drags down related industrial demand and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of months where HOUST falls below 1000, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond GDPC1 growth.
